name: Pico Mágina - circular through the Sierra Mágina natural park
name_original: Pico Mágina - circular por el parque natural de Sierra Mágina
region_intro: Hiking around Cambil offers access to the mountainous terrain of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park in Jaén, Spain. The region is characterized by significant elevation changes, with prominent peaks like Pico Mágina defining the landscape. Trails often traverse varied environments, including forested areas and open mountain slopes. This area provides a range of hiking experiences within a protected natural setting. Best hiking trails around Cambil The most popular hiking route is Pico Mágina - circular through the Sierra Mágina natural park, a 13.7 miles (22.0 km) trail that takes 7 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a comprehensive exploration of the natural park's high-altitude features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cerro Almadén Loop Trail, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route ascends Cerro Almadén, providing views of the surrounding mountainous landscape. Local hikers also love the Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m) – Miramundos Shelter loop from Cortijo de Vista Alegre, a 13.3 miles (21.5 km) trail leading through high mountain terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Cambil is defined by its mountainous peaks, natural park environments, and varied elevation. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Cambil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from nearly 100 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Cambil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cambil?
A: Cambil offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 30 trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, all within the mountainous terrain of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Cambil?
A: Hikers in Cambil will encounter significant elevation changes, characteristic of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park. The terrain varies from forested areas to open mountain slopes, often leading to prominent peaks like Pico Mágina. Expect a mix of challenging ascents and scenic descents.

Q: Are there easy hiking options for beginners in Cambil?
A: Yes, Cambil has several easy hiking options suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. There are 3 identified easy routes, providing gentle introductions to the region's natural beauty without demanding strenuous effort.

Q: What are some challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Cambil?
A: For experienced trekkers, Cambil offers challenging routes that explore high-altitude features. A notable difficult trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m) – Miramundos Shelter loop from Cortijo de Vista Alegre&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 13.3 miles (21.5 km) and typically takes over 7 hours to complete.

Q: How long do hikes around Cambil typically take?
A: The duration of hikes in Cambil varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987865932/cerro-almaden-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Almadén Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; takes around 3 hours 45 minutes, while more difficult routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983020420/pico-magina-circular-through-the-sierra-magina-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina - circular through the Sierra Mágina natural park&lt;/a&gt; can take over 7 hours.

Q: Are there any notable natural landmarks or viewpoints to discover?
A: Absolutely. The region around Cambil is rich in natural landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuadros River&lt;/a&gt;, visit the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua de Bedmar&lt;/a&gt;, or ascend to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit (2,167 m)&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5198952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adelfal de Cuadros Trail&lt;/a&gt; also offers a unique natural experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Cambil?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Cambil. Many trails are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e987865932/cerro-almaden-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Almadén Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a comprehensive experience of the area's mountainous landscape.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cambil?
A: The hiking trails in Cambil are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from nearly 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views from the peaks, and the immersive experience within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park.

Q: Is Cambil suitable for family-friendly hikes?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easy trails available that can be suitable for families. It's recommended to check the difficulty and length of specific routes to ensure they align with your family's capabilities. Look for shorter, less strenuous options for a pleasant family outing.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Cambil?
A: As Cambil is located within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, regulations regarding dogs can vary. Generally, dogs are permitted on trails but often need to be kept on a leash, especially in protected areas or during certain seasons to protect wildlife. Always check local signage or park guidelines before heading out with your dog.

Q: What are the parking options for trailheads in Cambil?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For popular starting points within the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, designated parking areas are often provided. It's advisable to research specific trailheads in advance or consult local information centers for the most accurate parking details.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cambil?
A: Given Cambil's mountainous environment, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher altitudes like Pico Mágina.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural points of interest near the trails?
A: Yes, beyond natural beauty, the area also features historical elements. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5298578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreón de Cuadros&lt;/a&gt;, a historic tower, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4129334" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albanchez de Mágina Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the region's past amidst your outdoor adventures.
tips: Summit located in the homonymous natural park in the province of Jaén. With its 2,167 m altitude, it is the highest peak in this province and is located on the dividing line between the municipalities of Albanchez de Mágina and Huelma.
